column3: Graph schema constants: node/relationship tables, primary keys, allowed fields, CSV filenames and column orders, Cypher DDL, and semantic-layer constants (`EMBEDDING_DIM`, `EMBEDDABLE_TABLES`, `HNSW_INDEX_NAMES`).
column4: Pure constants, no logic. Intentional duplication between `FIELD_MAP` (used for upsert validation) and `NODE_COLUMNS` (used for CSV column order) -- they serve different purposes and should not be collapsed. `embedding` is deliberately NOT in `FIELD_MAP`, `NODE_COLUMNS`, `CONCEPT_FIELDS`, or `SOURCE_FILE_FIELDS` even though every NODE_DDL and REL_DDL entry declares `, embedding FLOAT[768]`: this keeps `upsert_node` / `upsert_edge` validation rejecting `embedding=` from MCP callers and keeps embeddings out of `export_csv`. `EMBEDDABLE_TABLES = NODE_TABLES + REL_TABLES` (all seven); `HNSW_INDEX_NAMES` covers NODE_TABLES only because KuzuDB's CREATE_VECTOR_INDEX rejects rel tables. The relationship DDL fixes description + git_revision (+ embedding) as the only edge attributes; adding new attributes requires coordinated updates to DDL, CSV export, and MCP handler signatures.

column3: All KuzuDB (LadybugDB) operations: schema init + idempotent migration, node and edge upsert/delete, stats, read-only query, CSV export, crash-safe COW rebuild, HNSW index lifecycle, fail-safe embedding writes, and `semantic_search` (HNSW-first with brute-force fallback).
column4: Connection discipline is load-bearing: every public function uses `_opened(db_path, *, read_only=False)` which opens a fresh Database + Connection, LOADs the VECTOR extension, yields, then deterministically closes. This matters because (a) COW requires passing a temp path, (b) overlapping connections corrupted the WAL, and (c) once HNSW exists on a table, every write to that table needs VECTOR loaded. `rebuild_from_csv` now uses atomic COW: builds a fresh DB at a uuid-suffixed temp path, runs all COPY FROM there, then calls `commit_write` to atomically swap it in; on any error the temp files are cleaned up and the live DB is untouched (critical for `handle_theo_reload` inside a long-lived MCP server). `reindex_all` runs in three phases (RO-read, embed with no DB open, single writer drop/SET/recreate) and on SET failure mid-loop nulls the entire column (`MATCH (n:T) SET n.embedding = NULL`), leaving search loudly empty rather than silently wrong with partial results. HNSW cosine metric returns `distance = 1 - cosine_similarity`; the HNSW path normalizes with `1 - distance` to match `array_cosine_similarity` scores. `upsert_edge` uses MERGE + SET in separate statements (KuzuDB cannot bind params in SET alongside MERGE). `export_csv` emits headerless CSVs and explicitly projects non-embedding columns so the derived `embedding FLOAT[768]` stays DB-only. Two named sentinels (`_ERR_COL_PRESENT`, `_ERR_INDEX_ABSENT`) isolate brittle string-match checks for idempotent migration and index-drop helpers.

column3: Copy-on-write lifecycle for the KuzuDB file: `begin_write` copies DB+WAL to a uuid-suffixed temp path; `commit_write` atomically renames back; `abort_write` cleans up on failure.
column4: Atomicity depends on `os.rename` being atomic on the same filesystem — if `.theo/db/` is ever a mountpoint boundary from its parent, this breaks. A stale WAL from the original is explicitly unlinked during commit when the tmp has no WAL, to keep the pair consistent. No parent-directory fsync; the CSVs are the durable source of truth and the `.db` is rebuildable, so a crash mid-rename is tolerable.

column3: Git helpers: `head_commit()` shells out to `git rev-parse HEAD`; `find_theo_root()` walks upward looking for `.theo/config.json`.
column4: `find_theo_root` caps traversal at 100 levels and tracks visited directories to survive circular symlinks — this guards the MCP server against hangs. `head_commit` returns `None` rather than raising when git is unavailable, so stats and freshness flows degrade gracefully outside a repo.

column3: Typer app registering `use`, `serve`, `stats`, `reload`, `reindex`, and `ui` subcommands. Installed as the `theo` console script via `pyproject.toml`.
column4: Each subcommand's implementation is lazy-imported inside the handler -- keeps `theo --help` fast and means optional deps (Flask for ui, MCP for serve, fastembed for reindex) are only required when actually invoked. `no_args_is_help=True` on the Typer app.

column3: The MCP server. Loads `.theo/config.json`, ensures the DB is rebuilt (and embedding column migrated) when missing, pre-warms the embedding model, then registers eight FastMCP tools over stdio: `theo_stats`, `theo_query`, `theo_search`, `theo_reload`, `theo_upsert_node`, `theo_upsert_edge`, `theo_delete_node`, `theo_delete_edge`.
column4: Every write-path tool wraps a COW begin/commit/abort envelope and re-exports all CSVs on success for durable versioning. Handler functions (`handle_theo_*`) are extracted from FastMCP registration for testability. Embedding contract (changed): upserts no longer auto-index -- the agent's contract is batch-write then call `theo_reload` (MCP) or `theo reindex` CLI once to refresh embeddings; this removes a full HNSW drop/rebuild per upsert which was wasteful at batch scale. `handle_theo_reload` returns `{"status": "partial"}` (not `"ok"`) when structural rebuild succeeds but `reindex_all` fails, signaling the agent to retry before relying on `theo_search`. `_ensure_db` rebuilds from CSVs when the DB file is missing AND runs idempotent embedding-column migration on every start. `handle_theo_search` validates and clamps `top_k` ([1, 1000]) before f-string interpolation into Cypher LIMIT. Pre-warm: `prewarm_model()` is called at startup; failures are logged at ERROR but do not abort startup since non-embedding tools (`theo_stats`, `theo_query`) do not need the model. `theo_reload` runs inside MCP so the rebuild owns the DB handle -- no external process races with the live server.

column3: `theo stats` — prints node/edge counts and freshness (last indexed commit vs. current HEAD) to the terminal.
column4: Read-only path; does not mutate the DB or CSVs. Shares freshness semantics with the MCP `theo_stats` tool: `is_stale` when `last_indexed_commit` is unset or differs from git HEAD.

column3: `theo use` -- idempotent project initialisation: `.theo/` layout, `.mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, skill files under `.claude/` and `.agents/`, refreshed `AGENTS.md` section, `.gitignore` entry for `.theo/db/`.
column4: Text-based surgery for `.codex/config.toml` (regex header matching) and `AGENTS.md` (markdown heading matching) preserves unrelated user content on re-run. MCP registration embeds the absolute `project_dir` in the `theo serve` args so the server works regardless of the agent's cwd. Skill files are duplicated into `.claude/skills/theo/` and `.agents/skills/theo/` for cross-tool compatibility (Claude Code vs. Cursor/Codex). Preserves existing `config.json` on re-run to avoid clobbering `last_indexed_commit`. `AGENTS_MD_SECTION` is the single source of truth for the blurb injected into each project's AGENTS.md -- keep it aligned with the self-reflection protocol documented in SKILL.md.

column3: `theo ui` — Flask server rendering the graph with vis.js at 127.0.0.1:7777. Opens a browser tab automatically unless `--no-browser`.
column4: Large embedded HTML/CSS/JS template accounts for the file's size and the `E501` ruff exemption for this file. Every HTTP request opens a fresh read-only ladybug connection: the MCP server's COW atomic-rename means any cached DB handle would read stale data. Search endpoint is a stub returning empty results — semantic search will come later. Flask is an optional dep (`theo[ui]`); `_ensure_flask()` gives a helpful install hint when missing. `mypy` strict mode has `disallow_untyped_decorators = false` for this module because `flask.route` resolves to Any when Flask isn't installed in CI.

column3: Top-level package marker (`theo`). Intentionally empty — public API is the CLI, MCP tools, and UI; nothing is re-exported here.
CLI subpackage marker.
PEP 561 marker declaring the `theo` package ships inline type hints.
Skills subpackage; exists so `importlib.resources.files("theo.skills")` can locate the bundled markdown.
column4: Resource loading via `importlib.resources` only works because `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].include` in `pyproject.toml` explicitly adds the non-Python markdown files to the wheel. Remove that line and skill files disappear at runtime.

column3: The canonical skill instruction copied into each target project. Documents the graph schema, the eight MCP tools (`theo_stats`, `theo_query`, `theo_search`, `theo_reload`, `theo_upsert_node`, `theo_upsert_edge`, `theo_delete_node`, `theo_delete_edge`), session startup protocol, building/updating procedures, exploration and self-reflection protocols, and the `theo` trigger phrase.
column4: This is the prompt that actually drives coding-agent behavior -- changes here are effectively product changes to Theo. Must stay in sync with MCP tool signatures in `serve.py` (tool names, parameters, guardrails) or the agent will issue calls that don't match reality. The lens pipeline (architect -> interface designer -> dependency master -> criticality finder -> simplicity advocate) is the heart of the build/update loop. Two orthogonal protocols frame the agent's reading and writing behavior: Exploration (prefer `theo_search` for free-text/intent queries, `theo_query` for structural traversal, then grep) and Self-reflection (after any major exploration, register non-obvious discoveries in node/edge `notes`). The `AGENTS.md` blurb written by `theo use` mirrors this self-reflection cue.

column3: Shared pytest fixtures: `tmp_db` (just an initialised KuzuDB) and `tmp_theo_project` (full `.theo/` layout with DB, CSVs, and config).
column4: `tmp_theo_project` bypasses `theo use` and writes a minimal `config.json` directly — integration tests get a clean workspace without having to orchestrate the CLI.

column3: Unit tests for schema constants: table lists, PK map, field allowlists, DDL strings.
Unit tests for COW file lifecycle (begin/commit/abort, WAL handling).
Unit tests for `head_commit` and `find_theo_root`, including the circular-symlink guard.
Unit tests for the UI helpers (`_level_tier`, `_esc_html`, graph building, Flask routes).
Unit tests for the idempotent config-surgery helpers in `theo use` (TOML stripping, AGENTS.md section refresh, .mcp.json merging).
Integration tests against a real KuzuDB: schema init, upsert, delete, guardrails, CSV export/rebuild round-trip.
column4: Marked `integration` in `pyproject.toml` so CI can deselect with `-m 'not integration'` on cost-sensitive jobs.

column3: Integration tests that exercise the full COW envelope end-to-end against a live DB.
Integration tests for the MCP server handler functions (`handle_theo_*`) wiring COW, DB ops, and CSV export together.
End-to-end tests that shell out to the installed `theo` binary via subprocess.
column4: Marked `e2e` — slowest tier, depends on the CLI being on PATH.

column3: Tests package marker.
Unit-tests package marker.
Integration-tests package marker.
E2E-tests package marker.
Project metadata, dependencies (incl. fastembed for the always-on semantic layer), optional extras (`ui`, `dev`), console script entry point, ruff/mypy/pytest config.
column4: Two critical settings: (1) `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].include = ["src/theo/skills/theo/*.md"]` -- bundles skill markdown into the wheel so `importlib.resources` finds it at runtime; (2) `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` exempts `src/theo/cli/ui.py` from E501 because of its embedded HTML template. The strict-mode mypy override for `theo.cli.ui` accommodates Flask's optional-extra status in CI; the `fastembed.*` override exists only to silence missing-stubs (fastembed itself is a required runtime dep). Dependabot pins narrow version windows (often single-version) for `real-ladybug`, `mcp`, `typer`, `pytest`, `mypy`, `fastembed`; bumps land as individual PRs.

column3: User-facing docs: what Theo is, two-layer storage model, install via `uv tool install`, `theo use` quickstart, UI, dev workflow.
Instruction file for coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor). Tells the agent to use the theo skill when planning and updating the graph, and to register new discoveries in node/edge notes.
column4: Content under the `# Theo` heading is kept in sync by `theo use` (`_update_agents_md`), which strips and rewrites that section on each run. Source of truth is `AGENTS_MD_SECTION` in `src/theo/cli/use.py`.

column3: uv lockfile pinning all transitive dependencies.
Apache-2.0 license.
Git ignore rules. `theo use` idempotently appends `.theo/db/` to make the runtime DB gitignored.
Pre-commit hooks: ruff (lint + format), mypy (strict), markdownlint.
Markdownlint rules used by pre-commit.
GitHub Actions CI: lint, type-check, unit tests. Fast tier (no integration markers).
GitHub Actions integration workflow: integration and e2e tests that need a real KuzuDB and the installed CLI.
Dependabot config for dependency updates.
`theo reload` -- rebuild the runtime KuzuDB from the on-disk CSV files, then recompute embeddings. Use after manual edits to `.theo/*.csv` or pulling changes that touch them.
column4: Unconditionally rebuilds via `rebuild_from_csv` (now COW-safe: corrupt CSV leaves the live DB untouched), then runs `reindex_all` -- fastembed is a core dependency so no availability guard. Only tables with n > 0 embeddings are printed to reduce noise. Does NOT lock against a concurrently-running `theo serve`; for in-session reloads triggered by a coding assistant, prefer the `theo_reload` MCP tool which rebuilds inside the server process and has no external race.

column3: Embedding pipeline: fastembed singleton for the `nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5` model (768-dim), `embed_documents` / `embed_query` with nomic task prefixes, `make_node_text` / `make_edge_text` text builders, `prewarm_model()`, and `reset_model()`.
column4: fastembed is now a hard (core) dependency -- `is_available()` is gone. The lazy singleton in `_get_model` loads on first embed call (~2 s); `prewarm_model()` is the public hook for callers wanting to pay that cost eagerly at startup. `EMBEDDING_DIM` (768) now lives in `_schema`; `_embed` re-exports it via `__all__` for backwards-compatible imports. `embed_documents` skips model-load entirely for empty input -- the old code loaded unconditionally as an install check, no longer needed. Nomic's training requires `"search_document: "` prefix for indexable text and `"search_query: "` for queries; wrong prefix silently degrades recall. `make_node_text` concatenates description + notes with a separator; `make_edge_text` is description-only (rel tables have no `notes`). Both return `""` for empty rows so callers can skip embedding.

column3: One-liner helper that INSTALLs and LOADs the KuzuDB `VECTOR` extension on a given connection, swallowing "already loaded" errors so it can be called unconditionally.
column4: KuzuDB's HNSW functions (`CREATE_VECTOR_INDEX`, `QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX`, `DROP_VECTOR_INDEX`) and `array_cosine_similarity` all require the VECTOR extension. Crucially, once an HNSW index exists on a table, EVERY subsequent write to that table (including plain MERGE/SET on unrelated columns) needs the extension loaded on its connection -- not just the connection that created the index. That's why `_opened()` in `_db.py` calls `load_vector_ext` universally rather than only when an embedding operation is about to happen.

column3: `theo reindex` -- recomputes embeddings for every row in every embeddable table and rebuilds the HNSW indexes from scratch.
column4: The agent-facing contract for refreshing the semantic index after a batch of upserts. Also the right entry point when switching embedding models or migrating an older DB without an `embedding` column. fastembed is now a core dependency so the old `is_available()` guard is gone -- `reindex_all` always attempts embedding. Mirrors `reload.py`: loads config, resolves DB path, runs `migrate_embedding_column`, delegates to `_db.reindex_all`.

column3: Unit tests for the text-building helpers in `_embed.py` (`make_node_text`, `make_edge_text`). No model load -- these tests run in the fast tier even without fastembed installed.
column4: Model-dependent tests (`embed_documents`, `embed_query`) live in `tests/integration/test_semantic_search.py` behind the `integration` marker because they download an ~80 MB model on first run.

column3: Integration tests for the full semantic layer: fastembed round-trips, idempotent schema migration, HNSW index lifecycle, `semantic_search` ranking and table-filter behavior, `write_node_embedding` atomicity under failure, CSV-excludes-embedding invariant, and `reindex_all`.
column4: Marked `integration` and gated on `pytest.importorskip("fastembed")` because the nomic model downloads on first run. `TestWriteNodeEmbedding::test_mid_sequence_set_failure_preserves_hnsw` is the regression test for the drop-then-SET-then-recreate atomicity guarantee: if SET raises, the HNSW must still be back on exit so subsequent writes don't silently degrade to brute force. `TestExportExcludesEmbeddings` enforces that CSVs never gain the `embedding` column -- the most important invariant separating "source of truth" (CSV) from "derived cache" (DB-only embeddings).

column3: Integration tests for the `theo reindex` CLI: populates embeddings from scratch, exits cleanly when no `.theo/config.json` is present, and a subprocess invocation sanity-checking the console script end-to-end.
column4: Gated on fastembed. The subprocess test uses `shutil.which("theo")` and skips rather than failing when the CLI isn't on PATH -- keeps local dev flexible (`uv run pytest` vs. `uv tool install` differ here).

column3: Integration tests for `theo reload`: CSV-as-source-of-truth round-trip (mutations-only-in-DB are discarded), rebuild-when-DB-missing, hard-exit paths for missing config/CSVs, and a fastembed-gated test that reload re-populates embeddings after a DB wipe.
column4: Split out from the older `test_cow_integration.py` because the CSV round-trip is a distinct contract from COW atomicity. The fastembed-gated test exercises the `reindex_all` step wired into `cli/reload.run`.
